title: Exterminating and Pest Control Services

narrative.executive_view: Pest control is a durable service with bounded, useful AI operating leverage rather than an AI replacement story. Census 561710 covers exterminating and controlling structural pests, including fumigation, while excluding crop and forestry pest control [S1]. The investable opportunity is to automate office intake, scheduling, billing, documentation, route analytics and parts of sales/management around a technician-led route. Rollins reported 75% recurring service mix at year-end 2025, strong organic growth across residential, commercial and termite lines, and 26 acquisitions during the year [S3]. Those features support demand and buyability, but rich entry multiples and route-density execution leave little room for a sloppy roll-up.
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: The field visit remains the production bottleneck: technicians inspect, identify conditions, apply treatments, place devices, document compliance and manage customer trust. AI can reduce the surrounding labor through phone intake, scheduling, dispatch, route optimization, renewal processing, invoicing, bookkeeping, proposal follow-up and management reporting. FieldRoutes' 2025 survey of 1,025 pest-company leaders found 27% were using AI for business performance, while a much larger installed software base shows the path from isolated tools to integrated workflows [S2]. The mature role estimate replaces roughly one-fifth of the supplied wage bill, not the technician majority.
narrative.value_capture: Capture is structurally favorable because pricing is contracted directly with households and commercial clients rather than administered by a payer. Rollins says recurring services are normally monthly, bimonthly or quarterly under initial one-year contracts and accounted for 75% of its 2025 business [S3]. Dense routes, customer contracts, reputation and service quality can let an operator retain office and routing savings. Competition is still local and real, so some efficiency will be reinvested in response time, marketing and technician pay. I separate that moderate-to-good capture from terminal demand: licensing and physical service protect the product, but they do not by themselves prove margin retention.
narrative.adoption_timing: Current AI adoption is below half: 73% of respondents in the FieldRoutes survey were not using AI to boost performance in 2025, versus 82% a year earlier [S2]. Routing and field-service software provide the better historical analogue; the industry first digitized routes, invoices and payments, then layered analytics and remote monitoring [S7]. A three-year t50 estimate reflects an existing software distribution channel but also small-operator fragmentation, integration work and the fact that many early uses are marketing or drafting rather than headcount substitution.
narrative.consolidation_economics: The buyer field is deep. PCT has identified Rollins, Terminix, Massey, Arrow, Truly Nolen, Rentokil, Anticimex and Certus as acquisition-active, and its 2025 Top 100 coverage continues to highlight private-equity demand [S6][S7]. Rollins alone completed 94 acquisitions over 2023-2025 [S3]. A 2025 private-market compilation reports 6.8-6.9x EBITDA for $1-5 million commercial/residential operators and 8.2-8.3x for $5-10 million operators [S4]. I use 6.8x entry and 8.2x exit as segment-blended estimates, with a clear warning that source deal details are not disclosed and that owner compensation, recurring mix, route density and termite liabilities can overwhelm headline multiples.
narrative.terminal_demand: Paid demand is durable. The work protects structures, food-service sites, healthcare facilities and households from recurring biological problems, and restricted-use pesticide application requires certified human applicators under federal standards administered by states, territories or tribes [S8]. Rollins' 2025 organic growth and recurring-service base are direct evidence that the service remains valued [S3]. Smart monitoring can reduce empty trap checks and some commercial truck rolls [S9], but it shifts labor toward targeted inspection and intervention rather than eliminating all pest-control service. The downside is thinner visit frequency at sensor-heavy accounts, not wholesale product disappearance.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The deterministic n_band of 70 is a floor-like estimate because its generic 15.65% margin excludes many well-run 20-99 employee operators that may exceed 20% EBITDA. Conversely, premium pest-control multiples can tempt buyers to capitalize owner labor, underreserve termite retreat/repair obligations or pay for route density they have not yet created. The labor-share input mixes 2024 wages, a March 2026 benefits ratio and 2022 receipts, and the role mix is at five-digit 56171. Remote monitoring claims come from vendors, and the private-multiple compilation does not reveal transaction counts. Wildlife, fumigation, termite, residential recurring and compliance-heavy commercial accounts require separate underwriting.

sources.title: 2022 North American Industry Classification System Manual
sources.what_it_supports: Exact 561710 scope: control of birds, mosquitoes, rodents, termites and other pests, including fumigation, but excluding crop and forestry pest control.

sources.title: 2025 State of the Pest Industry: Key Findings and Actionable Strategies
sources.what_it_supports: Vendor-sponsored Thrive Analytics survey of 1,025 pest-company leaders: 73% were not using AI for business performance in 2025, improved from 82% in 2024; also documents broad software use and labor pressure.

sources.title: Rollins, Inc. 2025 Form 10-K
sources.what_it_supports: Public-company evidence on 75% recurring service mix, contract cadence, routing technology, strong 2025 demand and growth, and 94 acquisitions over three years including 26 in 2025.

sources.title: EBITDA Multiples for Pest Control Companies: 2025 Report
sources.what_it_supports: Private-market compilation reporting 6.8-6.9x EBITDA for $1-5 million commercial/residential pest companies and 8.2-8.3x for $5-10 million companies in Q1 2025.

sources.title: The Succession Cliff Report 2026
sources.what_it_supports: Modeled Census-based proxy that 49.5% of owners in broad NAICS sector 56 are age 55 or older; not a pest-specific or age-60 observation.

sources.title: PCT Announces 24th Annual Top 100 List
sources.what_it_supports: Current 2025 evidence of a large structural-pest market, Top 100 buyer landscape and continuing private-equity interest.

sources.title: Pest Management: All Grown Up
sources.what_it_supports: Named acquisition-active consolidators, historical digitization of routing and field documentation, route-software productivity evidence, recurring-revenue appeal and professional-service demand.

sources.title: Federal Certification Standards for Pesticide Applicators
sources.what_it_supports: Federal certification requirements for people applying or supervising restricted-use pesticides and the structural/health-related commercial applicator category.

sources.title: How smart rodent control technology saves PMPs money
sources.what_it_supports: Vendor evidence that connected rodent devices reduce empty-trap checking and service time; used only to frame remote-monitoring labor and visit-frequency risk.

labor_share.method: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 561710 ($8,715,666,468) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 561710 ($19,563,962,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

occupations.title: Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel
First-Line Supervisors of Housekeeping and Janitorial Workers
Secretaries and Administrative Assistants, Except Legal, Medical, and Executive
Pesticide Handlers, Sprayers, and Applicators, Vegetation
First-Line Supervisors of Office and Administrative Support Workers

n_band.derivation: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[56]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $139,825 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 561710); class <5: 8,882 firms, avg revenue/firm $236,662 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $37,038 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 2,491 firms, avg revenue/firm $736,951 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $115,333 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 1,373 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,587,316 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $248,415 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 766 firms, avg revenue/firm $4,665,939 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $730,219 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 70 firms, avg revenue/firm $21,941,586 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $3,433,858 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 21 firms, avg revenue/firm $396,989,381 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $62,128,838 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 70
n_band.margin_source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: The role-weighted estimate preserves the licensed, physical technician core while automating much of office administration, customer service, bookkeeping, dispatch, documentation, lead follow-up and some management analytics.
caveats: AI adoption evidence covers company use, not measured task-level labor displacement.
EPA certification applies to restricted-use pesticides, not every pest-control task; it supports a human floor but is not a universal licensing claim.
Remote monitoring can reduce field labor on some commercial rodent accounts; that demand effect is not used to inflate role automation.

breakdown_by_role.rationale: Physical inspection and treatment remain; AI mainly removes documentation and improves diagnosis or routing around the visit.
Scheduling, service records, renewals, invoice handling and routine data entry are highly automatable.
Branch reporting, pricing analysis and route dashboards automate, while hiring, safety accountability and local relationships persist.

breakdown_by_role.role: Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Lead handling, quoting and follow-up automate, but inspections, door-to-door work and complex commercial selling stay human.

breakdown_by_role.role: First-Line Supervisors of Housekeeping and Janitorial Workers
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Routing analytics can widen span of control, but field training, safety and service-quality supervision remain physical and accountable.
Routine calls, appointment changes and FAQ handling automate, with infestations, cancellations and sensitive escalations retained.

breakdown_by_role.role: Secretaries and Administrative Assistants, Except Legal, Medical, and Executive
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Document preparation, scheduling and administrative coordination are highly automatable.

breakdown_by_role.role: Pesticide Handlers, Sprayers, and Applicators, Vegetation
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Pesticide application is physical field work; only planning and paperwork around it automate.

breakdown_by_role.role: First-Line Supervisors of Office and Administrative Support Workers
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Queue control, monitoring and reporting automate more than coaching, exception approval and staffing.
Reconciliation, invoice coding and routine bookkeeping automate, while controls and unusual transactions persist.
The small residual is assumed to contain mostly additional field and specialist work with a bounded administrative component.

pi_dist.rationale: Direct customer contracts and recurring local pricing let operators retain most office and routing savings, though competition and customer-service reinvestment pass part of the gain through.
caveats: Rollins is a scaled public operator, so its contract mix and pricing discipline may overstate retention at independent targets.

pi_moat.rationale: Recurring contracts, route density, local reputation, technician service quality and compliance documentation create switching friction, offset by fragmentation and low entry barriers.
caveats: Moat is strongest in dense recurring and compliance-heavy commercial routes and weakest in one-time residential work.

t50_years.rationale: AI use rose from 18% to 27% in one year and can distribute through established field-service platforms, but fragmented operators and integration constraints make an immediate majority unlikely.
caveats: Company use is broader than effective adoption of labor-replacing workflows, so extrapolating the recent one-year change would be too aggressive.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: Derived as one minus the 73% of surveyed pest companies reporting no AI use to boost business performance in 2025.
caveats: Vendor-sponsored company survey; any AI use does not necessarily imply material effective adoption or headcount substitution.

historical_analogs.value: Cloud field-service software, route optimization, mobile payments and electronic commercial documentation diffused over two decades; routing produced measurable productivity before generative AI arrived.
historical_analogs.rationale: The analogue shows that pest operators adopt technology when it improves route economics, but small-company diffusion and workflow change are gradual.
historical_analogs.plausible_range: AI could diffuse faster through existing platforms, or remain an add-on if integrations and service quality disappoint.
caveats: Legacy routing adoption is not identical to autonomous customer-service or back-office AI.

owners_60plus_pct.rationale: Broad NAICS 56 has a modeled 49.5% share of owners age 55+; 35% over 60 is a conservative downward bridge for pest-control employer firms.
caveats: This is a sector-56, age-55+ Census-based proxy transformed to age 60+, not a direct 561710 observation.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: The reopened evidence supports an aging broad-sector owner base and active acquisition demand but does not directly document a U.S. 561710 succession shortage, so the boolean remains false.
caveats: Absence of sufficiently specific reopened evidence is not evidence that succession shortages do not exist.

active_consolidators.rationale: PCT names eight acquisition-active strategic or PE-backed groups, while Rollins' 26 acquisitions in 2025 and current Top 100 coverage confirm continuing activity.
caveats: The named-buyer list is partly from 2021; current activity is confirmed for the market and Rollins, not individually reverified for every named group.

buy_mult.rationale: The selected entry multiple matches the report's $1-5 million EBITDA commercial average and is close to its residential average, appropriate for a mixed recurring structural-pest target.
caveats: The compilation does not disclose transaction count or deal-level observations; exact mix, recurring share, owner dependence and geography matter.
Normalize owner compensation: subscale market quotes may be SDE rather than EBITDA.

exit_mult.rationale: The selected exit multiple equals the report's $5-10 million EBITDA commercial average and sits just below its residential average, representing a scaled mixed platform without assuming public-company valuation.
caveats: Exit multiple expansion requires actual scale, retention and route density; market compression can eliminate it.
The same undisclosed-sample limitation affects both buy and exit benchmarks.

pricing_structure.value: Primarily recurring residential and commercial service contracts billed monthly, bimonthly or quarterly, plus one-time treatments and annually renewed termite monitoring or warranties; prices are negotiated directly with customers.
pricing_structure.rationale: Direct recurring contracts support retention of internal savings, while one-time work and competitive local markets remain more price sensitive.
pricing_structure.plausible_range: From recurring route-heavy books to one-time, seasonal or termite-heavy specialists
caveats: Rollins' 75% recurring mix is a scaled-company benchmark, not an industry-wide observed average.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: The mature role estimate is distinct from timing: 27% current company-level AI use and a three-year t50 phase the labor-cost uplift rather than treating it as immediately available.

terminal_value.justification: AI does not physically inspect and treat most structures; recurring biological demand, commercial compliance and credentialed pesticide work preserve paid service, while remote monitoring only pressures selected visit frequency.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: The owner-age estimate is explicitly a broad sector-56 proxy, the private multiple report is an undisclosed-sample compilation, and Rollins is a scaled-company proxy for recurring mix and route economics.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Direct contracts permit substantial retention, but pi_dist and pi_moat reduce capture for local competition and service reinvestment; physical/credential demand is used only for terminal durability, not capture.

dataset_mismatch.description: Labor share combines 2024 QCEW wages, a March 2026 benefits ratio and 2022 SUSB receipts before harmonization; roles are at five-digit 56171 while firm counts are six-digit 561710.
sensitivity: Different receipt or compensation vintages can move labor share and therefore V without changing the role judgments.
The 15.65% generic margin makes n_band 70 a floor-like estimate; at 20%+ margins, part of the 766-firm 20-99 class could enter the band.
Five-digit role weights may blend neighboring activities and should be replaced by target payroll in diligence.

market_boundary.dominant_subsegment: Recurring residential and commercial structural-pest routes
market_boundary.selection_basis: The dominant selection reflects the contracted route model evidenced by Rollins and the target's likely mixed structural-pest book; crop/forestry pest control is excluded by Census and vegetation/lawn application overlap is treated cautiously.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: Moves A from 5.0 to 2.0 and materially changes timing of realized value.
Moves mature labor uplift because technician tasks dominate the wage mix.
Changes buyability B; current evidence is only a broad-sector age proxy.
Changes M and whether scale arbitrage compensates for a premium 6.8x entry.

reviewer_note: Golden attempt 1. Exact six-digit scope and both EBITDA multiple selections were checked against reopened sources. The succession boolean is conservatively false because no sufficiently specific U.S. 561710 shortage source was retained.
